label: has growth temperature optimum
definition: The optimal temperature at which an organism achieves maximum growth rate (in degrees Celsius).

label: has growth temperature minimum
definition: The minimum temperature at which an organism can grow (in degrees Celsius).

label: has growth temperature maximum
definition: The maximum temperature at which an organism can grow (in degrees Celsius).

label: has NaCl concentration optimum
definition: The optimal NaCl concentration for growth expressed as weight/volume percentage.

label: has NaCl concentration minimum
definition: The minimum NaCl concentration tolerated for growth (w/v percentage).

label: has NaCl concentration maximum
definition: The maximum NaCl concentration tolerated for growth (w/v percentage).

label: has pH optimum
definition: The optimal pH for growth on the pH scale (0-14).

label: has pH minimum
definition: The minimum pH at which growth can occur.

label: has pH maximum
definition: The maximum pH at which growth can occur.

label: assimilates
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical entity, where the organism takes up and incorporates the chemical into its biomass or metabolic intermediates.
notes: 266 unique chemical traits; broader than carbon source

label: uses as energy source
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical entity, where the organism oxidizes or catabolizes the chemical primarily for energy (ATP) generation.
notes: 97 unique chemical traits; distinct from carbon source

label: uses as nitrogen source
definition: A relation between an organism and a nitrogen-containing chemical, where the organism assimilates the nitrogen for biosynthesis.
notes: 57 unique traits; may already exist as METPO:2000014

label: uses as electron donor
definition: A relation between an organism and a chemical entity, where the organism oxidizes the chemical to obtain electrons for energy metabolism.

label: cell shape
definition: A phenotypic quality that describes the geometric shape of a bacterial or archaeal cell.
notes: Values: rod, coccus, spiral, filamentous, etc.

label: cell length
definition: A phenotypic quality that describes the length of a bacterial or archaeal cell, typically measured in micrometers.
addresses_traits: cell length, cell length minimum, cell length maximum

label: cell width
definition: A phenotypic quality that describes the width or diameter of a bacterial or archaeal cell, typically measured in micrometers.
addresses_traits: cell width, cell width minimum, cell width maximum

label: cell color
definition: A phenotypic quality that describes the color or pigmentation of bacterial or archaeal cells.
addresses_traits: cell color, cell color: yellow pigment
notes: Values: yellow, white, cream, orange, red, etc.

label: flagellar arrangement
definition: A phenotypic quality that describes the arrangement pattern of flagella on a cell.

label: GC content percentage
definition: A genomic quality that describes the percentage of guanine-cytosine base pairs in the genome.

label: genome size
definition: A genomic quality that describes the total size of the genome in base pairs.

label: gene count
definition: A genomic quality that describes the total number of genes in the genome.

label: coding density
definition: A genomic quality that describes the percentage of the genome that codes for proteins.

label: oxygen requirement
definition: A phenotypic quality that describes the oxygen requirement for growth.

label: pH tolerance range
definition: An environmental quality that describes the range of pH values that support growth.
notes: Use with Phase 1 pH data properties

label: pH minimum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality that describes the minimum pH value that supports growth.

label: pH maximum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality that describes the maximum pH value that supports growth.

label: pH optimum
definition: An environmental quality that describes the optimal pH value for growth.

label: temperature tolerance range
definition: An environmental quality that describes the range of temperatures that support growth.
notes: Use with Phase 1 temperature data properties

label: temperature minimum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality that describes the minimum temperature that supports growth.

label: temperature maximum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality that describes the maximum temperature that supports growth.

label: temperature optimum
definition: An environmental quality that describes the optimal temperature for growth.

label: salinity tolerance range
definition: An environmental quality that describes the range of salt concentrations that support growth.
notes: Use with Phase 1 NaCl data properties

label: salinity minimum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality that describes the minimum salinity/NaCl concentration that supports growth.

label: salinity maximum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality that describes the maximum salinity/NaCl concentration that supports growth.

label: salinity optimum
definition: An environmental quality that describes the optimal salinity/NaCl concentration for growth.

label: indole production capability
definition: A phenotypic capability describing the production of indole from tryptophan via tryptophanase.

label: methyl red test positive
definition: A phenotypic quality indicating a positive methyl red test (mixed acid fermentation).

label: hemolytic activity
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the ability to lyse red blood cells (alpha/beta/gamma hemolysis).

label: selective media growth capability
definition: A phenotypic capability describing the ability to grow on selective or differential media.
addresses_traits: growth: MacConkey agar, growth: blood agar, growth: bile acid susceptible

label: bile resistance
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the ability to grow in the presence of bile acids or bile salts.

label: biosafety level classification
definition: A risk assessment quality describing the biosafety level (BSL-1 to BSL-4) assigned to an organism.
